titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Rex Beach Papers
titlestmt.author: by Leigh Golden

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday May 27, 2020 at 04:20

did.unittitle: Rex Beach papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Box 5: Restricted fragile material. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
Box 6 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Lord Durham Rare Books Inc. on the William Robertson Coe Fund No. 3, 2018.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Writings and interviews, 1905-1946. II. Promotional materials and ephemera related to movie adaptations, 1914-1956. III. Correspondence, photographs, and ephemera, 1908-1942.

bioghist.head: Rex Beach
bioghist.p: Rex Ellingwood Beach was born in 1877. Beach wrote numerous novels and short stories about the American frontier including The Silver Horde (1909). Many of Beach's novels were adapted into films, the most notable being The Spoilers , which was adapted five different times. Beach died in 1949.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Rex Beach Papers. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The Rex Beach Papers, which span from 1905-1956, contain correspondence, photographs, printed material, and other papers by or relating to Rex Ellingwood Beach's life and work.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Rex Beach Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: Writings and interviews

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this series roughly maintain the order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings by Beach in numerous publications.

did.unittitle: Advertisement, "Told in the Storm" - Smith's Magazine, vol. 10, no. 4
"The Pain-Killer Revisited" - Hearst's International Cosmopolitan, vol. 97, no. 4
"The Place is Alaska; The Business is Gold Mining" - Hearst's International Cosmopolitan, vol. 100, no. 1
"When the Mail Came In: A Rugged Romance of the North" - The World's Greatest Stories, vol. 1, no. 2
"Beyond Control" - Liberty magazine, vol. 9, no. 32
The Ne'er-Do-Well. A serial Story" - Everybody's Magazine, vol. 24, no. 6
"Captain Innocencio" - McClure's Magazine, vol. 36, no. 4
"Sons of the Gods" - Hearst's International Cosmopolitan, vol. 85, no. 4
"Red Cheeks" - Hearst's International Cosmopolitan, vol. 84, no. 4
"Gold in Them Hills" (condensed from Hearst's International Cosmopolitan) - Reader's Digest, vol. 29, no. 176
"The Iron Trail. A Serial Story" - Everybody's Magazine, vol. 29, no. 3
"The Author and the Film" - The Mentor, vol. 9, No. 6
"Class Will Tell" - Liberty magazine, vol. 23, no. 28
"Padlocked: The Story of a Sensitive Girl; A Novel of Today's Morals," part II - Hearst's International Cosmopolitan, vol. 89, no. 6
"Modern Miracle Men: Dr. James S. Greene, Who Cures People of Stuttering" - Hearst's International Cosmopolitan, vol. 102, no. 1
"The World in His Arms" - Hearst's International Cosmopolitan, vol. 120, no. 1
"Son of the Gods" - Screen Book, vol. 4, no. 4
"Alaska's Flying Frontiersmen" - The American Magazine, vol. 121, no. 4
"How and Why We Ought to Swim" - The Saturday Evening Post, vol. 178, no. 7
"With Bridges Burned" - Collier's, Easter Issue, vol. 44, no. 26
"Rex Beach Explains How to Get Back Vim and Energy When 'Played Out'" - Esquire magazine
"The Weight of Obligation" - The Sun, The Semi-Monthly Magazine, section 5
"The Colonel and the Horse-Thief" - The Saturday Evening Mail (New York)
Promotional materials and ephemera related to movie adaptations

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this series roughly maintain the order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes advertisements, flyers, posters, booklets and other promotional material related to film adaptations of Rex Beach writings.

did.unittitle: "The Spoilers," Universal-International, color horizontal advertising banner
"The Avengers," Republic Pictures, black and white photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Based on "Don Careless," by Rex Beach.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Based on "The Net," by Rex Beach.

Correspondence, photographs, and ephemera

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this series roughly maintain the order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, photographs, and other ephemera related to Rex Beach's life and work.

did.unittitle: Color cigar box labels of Rex Beach, Henry Kolbe, Chicago Illinois
"The Barrier," Harper's Magazine Advertiser, advertisements
Black and white photographic portrait of Rex Beach, Moffett Studio, Chicago
Envelope, in Rex Beach's hand, addressed to Dr. M. J. Eisenberg

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a pasted-in photoprint of Rex Beach receiving the LL.D. from the Philadelphia School of Osteopathy.

did.unittitle: Black and white photographs (on one sheet) of Wiley Post, Rex Beach, Joe Crosson and Will Rogers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs taken in Juneau, Alaska, in August 1935 and then printed after the deaths of Wiley Post and Will Rogers in a 15 August 1935 plane-crash.

did.unittitle: Black and white photograph of Rex Beach, Grantland Rice and Sewell Ford, golfing at Palm Beach
"When Does the Author Smile?," black and white photograph of Rex Beach with fish, Amethyst Lake, Canada
"The Ne'er-Do-Well," Harper's Magazine Advertiser, advertisement
Beach, Rex to Dr. Hamilton Holt, typed letter
Beach, Rex to the Chief of the Sebring Fire Department, Florida, typed letter
Beach, Rex to Edward C. Higgins, handwritten letter
Beach, Rex to John G. Thomas, typed letter
Beach, Rex to various publishers, mimeograph form letter
Beach, Rex to Carlyle Ellis, typed letter on National War Work Council stationary
"The Net. The Contessa," Weekly Kansas City Star, promotional publication
"The Silver Horde," The Evening Bulletin, promotional advanced publication

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also contains a black and white postcard, "'The Silver Horde,' Cordova, Alaska."

did.unittitle: Beach, Rex to John E. Nitchie, typed letter
Beach, Rex to Marian C. Deane, typed letter
Black and white photograph of Rex Beach at testimonial dinner, Rollins College Alumni Association
Black and white photograph of Rex Beach and Grantland Rice, Artists and Writers Golf Tournament
Black and white photograph of Rex Beach and Fred Painton, Artists and Writers Golf Tournament
Black and white photographic portrait of Rex Beach

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains note on back stating that Rex Beach and David W. King have purchased the Morris County Press.

did.unittitle: Poctcard of Rex Beach's cabin in Rampart, Alaska

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rex Beach wrote "The Barrier" within this cabin. Postcard is addressed "Rex Beach to Mrs. Gatet.

did.unittitle: Black and white photographic portrait of Rex Beach, "Ref. Dept. N.E.A."
Black and white photographic portrait of Rex Beach, "Author in Goldwyn Pictures"
Seagram's 5 and 7 Crown Whiskey advertisement featuring Rex Beach, "Yes, it's Smoother Today"
Black and white photograph of Rex Beach receiving an honorary degree, Philadelphia College of Osteopathy
Postcard of Rex Beach's cabin in Rampart, Alaska

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rex Beach wrote "The Barrier" within this cabin. Postcard is addressed to Miss Elizabeth Studer from S. F. Milner.

did.unittitle: Harper & Brothers advertisement card for "Flowing Gold"
Advertising postcards announcing publication of Rex Beach books

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Companies highlighted on postcards include W. B. Clarke Company, the Korner & Wood Company, and the Emery, Bird, Thayer Dry Goods Company.

did.unittitle: Advertising postcards for "The Iron Trail" and "The Ne'er-Do-Well"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Company highlighted on postcards is the J. R. Weldin & Company.

did.unittitle: Black and white photograph of Rex Beach
Photogravure of Rex Beach wearing riding boots, a jacket and hat

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains biographical information on Rex Beach, one of the "Makers of Modern American Fiction."

did.unittitle: Typed letter from Rex Beach to Orson Lovell
Watcher, Dr. Joseph V., Rex Beach and Dr. Josephine E. Tilden, From My Note Book / Food From Under the Sea / Comment on Mr. Rex Beach's Article, pamphlet
Beach, Rex. Modern Miracle Men: An Article. Washington D.C. United States Government Printing Office
The Authors' League of America, Inc. Membership List, New York
Postcard of golfers, Harder Hall, Sebring, Florida, "On Rex Beach Lake - The Orange Blossom Trail"
Postcards featuring Dr. M. W. Locke
Beach, Rex to N. Stuart Watts, handwritten letter
"King Battler of the Deep, as told by Rex Beach," newspaper clipping of full page cartoon Camel cigarette advertisement
"Rex Beach Stories," color poster advertisement for Western novels
